task_name: HTML Bank Statement with Hidden Interest Income Gap
broad_objective: Prepare a simple federal tax return for a single filer with W-2 income. The client provided their bank statement as an HTML export from Chase Online Banking. Identify any missing tax documents needed for a complete return.

provided_files.description: Structured W-2 data with wages, withholding, and 401(k) contribution in Box 12
HTML-format Chase bank statement showing deposits, debits, transfers, and running balance for December 2025.

processing_challenges.description: HTML bank statement with styled tables. Agent must parse the DOM to extract transactions and identify any interest credits that would trigger a 1099-INT requirement.
processing_challenges.extraction_target: Transaction list, any interest payments, deposit sources to verify against W-2

processing_challenges.description: Standard JSON W-2. Agent must extract wages and identify 401(k) contribution for Saver's Credit evaluation.

missing_artifacts.artifact_name: Form 1099-INT if bank account earned interest
missing_artifacts.why_non_derivable: The HTML bank statement may show interest credits. If the account earned $10+ in interest during the year, Chase would issue a 1099-INT. The December statement alone cannot confirm the full-year interest total. The agent should flag this as a potential missing document.
missing_artifacts.detection_signal: Bank statement is from a checking account. If any 'Interest Payment' transactions appear, the agent should ask about the 1099-INT. Even if no interest appears in December, the account may have earned interest in other months.
missing_artifacts.retrieval_method: Ask the client if they received any 1099-INT from Chase or check their Chase online tax documents.

gold_retrieval_order: Form 1099-INT if bank account earned interest
why_context_tempts_premature_progress: The W-2 provides all the data needed for a basic return with standard deduction. The bank statement seems supplementary. A weak agent will compute the return from the W-2 alone without checking the bank statement for interest income signals.
what_bad_model_does: Ignores the HTML bank statement entirely or parses it but doesn't check for interest income transactions. Proceeds with W-2-only return.
what_good_model_does: Parses the HTML bank statement, scans transactions for interest credits, and asks the client about full-year interest income and whether a 1099-INT was received from Chase.
scoring_notes: Tests html_parse skill in a simple context. The key insight is that the agent must parse the HTML and check for interest transactions, not just ignore a non-PDF bank statement.
